expressions.english: something to eat; food
expressions.notes:
것 = thing, stuff; object
먹을 것 literally means "thing to eat," but it is often used as a set expression that refers to food in general.

expressions.english: to eat something; to have a meal
I ate.
What did you eat?
expressions.notes:
뭐 = what; something
뭐 is the contracted form of 무엇.

expressions.english: What do you want to eat?
Let's eat together.
Let's go eat.
Can I eat this?
expressions.notes:
이거 = this thing; this
-이/어/여도 되다 = to be okay to + V

expressions.english: Who ate all of this?
expressions.notes:
누가 = who; someone (subject)
누가 is the subject form of 누구.
다 = all; every (thing)

expressions.english: Nobody wants to eat?
expressions.notes:
아무도 = nobody; anybody (always used with a negative sentence structure)
안 = not

expressions.english: Please give me something to eat.
expressions.notes:
좀 = a little; please
주다 = to give
-(으)세요 = (polite) imperative

expressions.english: I came here to buy something to eat.
expressions.notes:
먹을 거 = food (the same as 먹을 것)
사다 = to buy
-(으)려 왔다 = to come to + V

expressions.english: I can't eat spicy food that well.
expressions.notes:
맵다 = to be spicy; to be hot
매운 = the adjective form of 맵다
거 = thing, stuff; object (the same as 것)
잘 = well; carefully
못 = can't

expressions.english: I fell asleep as soon as I ate.
expressions.notes:
-자마자 = as soon as + S + V
잠들다 = to fall asleep

expressions.english: There is nothing to eat at home.
expressions.notes:
집 = house, home
없다 = to not have; to not be there; to not exist

expressions.english: I'm full, so I don't want to eat.
expressions.notes:
배 = stomach, belly
부르다 = to feel full
-고 싶지 않다 = to not want to + V

expressions.english: I ate too much, so I'm full.
expressions.notes:
너무 = too much, excessively; very
많다 = to be a lot
많이 = is an adverb form of 많다.
배 = stomach, belly

expressions.english: Tomorrow I am going to eat dinner with my friend.
expressions.notes:
내일 = tomorrow
친구 = friend
저녁 = evening; dinner
-(으)ㄹ 것이다 = will; to be going to + V

expressions.english: Don't play with your food.
expressions.notes:
음식 = food
먹는 음식 is a commonly used set expression that refers to food.
가지고 = with; using
장난치다 = to play with something; to play a trick/prank
-지 마세요 = (polite) Don't + V

expressions.korean: 너무 맛이 없어서 먹을 수가 없어요.
expressions.english: It tastes really bad, so I can't eat it.
expressions.notes:
맛 = taste
맛이 없다 = to be not tasty; to taste bad
-(으)ㅂ 수 없다 = to be unable to + V, can't
